Back then
Commute
I grew up in Butantan.
I was sitting in a traffic jam when I decided to leave.
It was raining, and the radio was playing something sad.
Two months later, we sold the apartment.
So you have always lived in the countryside? Or did you move there later?
No, later. I grew up in Butantan, in Sao Paulo. My whole childhood was there.
What was it like back then?
Very noisy and very crowded. I commuted two hours a day. But there were good things too.
So what made you finally leave?
One afternoon I was sitting in a traffic jam downtown, and I just decided. Two months later we sold everything.
I grew up in Butantan, in Sao Paulo.
Back then, the neighborhood was noisy and crowded.
I used to commute two hours a day.
One afternoon I was sitting in traffic, and I decided to leave.
I miss some of it, but I would not go back.

I grew up in a small apartment above my grandmother's bakery. Back then the street was full of children, and we played outside until it got dark. My mother was always working, so my grandmother raised me, really. I remember the smell of bread every single morning. The neighborhood is completely different now. The bakery closed in two thousand and four, and there is a bank there today. Last year I walked past the building while I was visiting the city, and honestly, I did not recognize anything except the door.

I remember the exact day I decided to leave. It was a Tuesday, and it was raining. I was sitting in a traffic jam downtown, and I was not even angry anymore. I was just tired. The radio was playing something I did not like, and I looked at the clock and I realized I had been in that car for one hour and forty minutes. And I thought, this is my life. I am spending my life here, in this car. Two months later we sold the apartment. People said I was crazy. I was not crazy. I was just done.

First, Tom asks Ana where she grew up. Then Ana says she spent her childhood in Butantan, in Sao Paulo. Next, she describes what the neighborhood was like back then. After that, she tells him about the day she was sitting in traffic and decided to leave. Finally, Tom says he did something very similar, and they laugh about it.

I used to commute two hours a day.
One afternoon I was sitting in traffic, and I decided to leave.
I miss some of it, but I would not go back.
